title: Early Foundations
subtitle: Your body begins its quiet transformation
description: In week 2, your body is preparing for ovulation and potential fertilization. While pregnancy has not yet begun, this phase plays an important role in setting the stage for conception.
what_to_expect: Ovulation may occur during this time depending on your cycle
Hormonal activity increases to support egg release
Your body prepares the uterine lining for possible implantation
care_tips: Maintain a balanced and nutritious diet
Stay hydrated and follow a regular sleep schedule
Avoid smoking, alcohol, and unnecessary medications

medical_checkups.note: This phase focuses on preparation and awareness rather than confirmation.

commonly_suggested.name: Ovulation Tracking
commonly_suggested.purpose: Helps identify the most fertile period

commonly_suggested.name: Preconception Consultation
commonly_suggested.purpose: Guides planning and ensures readiness for pregnancy

medical_checkups.guidance: Your doctor may suggest additional guidance based on your health history and cycle regularity.

important_note: Healthy habits during this phase can positively influence early pregnancy development.
reassurance: Even before pregnancy begins, your body is working in preparation for the journey ahead.
medical_disclaimer: Always consult a healthcare professional before making significant lifestyle or medical decisions.

diabetes.personalized_description: Maintaining stable blood sugar levels during this phase can support better reproductive health.
diabetes.medical_focus: Blood glucose control may be reviewed when planning for pregnancy.
diabetes.reassurance: Stable habits now can support a healthier start ahead.

anemia.personalized_description: Improving iron levels before pregnancy can support better energy and overall health.
care_tips: Include iron-rich foods in your diet
Pair iron sources with vitamin C for better absorption
anemia.medical_focus: Hemoglobin levels may be assessed as part of preconception care.
anemia.reassurance: Building strength early supports your body for the upcoming changes.

hypertension.personalized_description: Managing blood pressure early helps create a stable environment for future pregnancy.
hypertension.medical_focus: Blood pressure may be evaluated during routine health checks.
hypertension.reassurance: Consistent care can help you stay balanced and prepared.

thyroid.personalized_description: Balanced thyroid function supports hormonal stability and reproductive health.
care_tips: Maintain consistent meal and sleep routines
thyroid.medical_focus: Thyroid levels may be reviewed when planning for pregnancy.
thyroid.reassurance: Keeping your routine steady helps your body stay in balance.

multi_condition_blend.combined_message: Managing multiple health factors during this preparatory phase can feel complex, but focusing on consistency can make it manageable.
priority_tips: Maintain regular meal and sleep schedules
Avoid sudden lifestyle or dietary changes
multi_condition_blend.reassurance: Small, steady efforts now can help create a strong foundation for the journey ahead.
